title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat, potentially leading to service disruptions, data loss, and financial damage. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can infiltrate cloud environments and deploy malicious code at scale.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and monitor CI/CD pipelines for unauthorized changes or suspicious activity.
Regularly back up critical data and ensure backups are stored offline or in isolated environments to prevent ransomware encryption.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions and conduct regular security audits of cloud environments.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Tiandy Easy7 Integrated Management Platform 7.17.0. The...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Tiandy Easy7 Integrated Management Platform 7.17.0. The affected element is an unknown function of the file /rest/devStatus/queryResources of the component Endpoint. Performing a manipulation of the argument areaId results in sql injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to perform SQL injection, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or manipulation of sensitive information stored in the database. Public exploit availability increases the likelihood of widespread attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization for the 'areaId' parameter to mitigate SQL injection risks.
Deploy web application firewalls (WAFs) to monitor and block malicious SQL injection attempts.
Contact the vendor and monitor for any patches or updates addressing CVE-2026-4287.

title: YAML::Syck versions through 1.36 for Perl has several potential security vulnerabilities...
description:
YAML::Syck versions through 1.36 for Perl has several potential security vulnerabilities including a high-severity heap buffer overflow in the YAML emitter.

The heap overflow occurs when class names exceed the initial 512-byte allocation.

The base64 decoder could read past the buffer end on trailing newlines.

strtok mutated n->type_id in place, corrupting shared node data.

A memory leak occurred in syck_hdlr_add_anchor when a node already had an anchor. The incoming anchor string 'a' was lea

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerabilities in YAML::Syck through version 1.36 for Perl, including a heap buffer overflow, memory corruption, and memory leaks, could lead to potential remote code execution, data corruption, or application crashes. These issues pose a significant risk, especially for applications relying on YAML parsing for critical operations.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of YAML::Syck or switch to a more secure YAML parser library.
Conduct a security review of applications using YAML::Syck to identify potential exploitation vectors.
Implement input validation and sanitization measures to mitigate the risk of malformed YAML data being processed.

title: Mattermost versions 10.11.x <= 10.11.10 fail to properly validate permission requirements in the...
description: Mattermost versions 10.11.x <= 10.11.10 fail to properly validate permission requirements in the team member roles API endpoint which allows team administrators to demote members to guest role. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2025-00531

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows team administrators to improperly demote members to a guest role due to insufficient permission validation in the team member roles API endpoint. While the severity is low, it could lead to unauthorized privilege changes and potential disruption of team workflows.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Mattermost to a version higher than 10.11.10 to patch the vulnerability.
Review and audit team administrator permissions to ensure proper access control.
Monitor team member role changes for any unauthorized modifications.

title: ZwickRoell Test Data Management versions prior to 3.0.8 contain a local file inclusion (LFI)...
description: ZwickRoell Test Data Management versions prior to 3.0.8 contain a local file inclusion (LFI) vulnerability in the /server/node_upgrade_srv.js endpoint. An unauthenticated attacker can supply directory traversal sequences via the firmware parameter to access arbitrary files on the server, leading to information disclosure of sensitive system files.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker to exploit a local file inclusion (LFI) flaw, potentially accessing sensitive system files. This could lead to information disclosure, aiding further attacks such as privilege escalation or lateral movement within the network.
recommended_actions: Immediately update ZwickRoell Test Data Management to version 3.0.8 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the affected endpoint (/server/node_upgrade_srv.js) using network-level controls, such as firewalls or access control lists (ACLs).
Conduct a thorough review of system logs to identify any unauthorized access attempts or exploitation of the vulnerability.

title: Buffalo TeraStation NAS TS5400R firmware version 4.02-0.06 and prior contain an excessive file...
description: Buffalo TeraStation NAS TS5400R firmware version 4.02-0.06 and prior contain an excessive file permissions vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to read the /etc/shadow file by uploading and executing a PHP file through the webserver. Attackers can exploit world-readable permissions on /etc/shadow to retrieve hashed passwords for all configured accounts including root.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-29516.

title: An Incorrect Access Control vulnerability exists in INDEX-EDUCATION PRONOTE prior to 2025.2.8....
description: An Incorrect Access Control vulnerability exists in INDEX-EDUCATION PRONOTE prior to 2025.2.8. The affected components (index.js and composeUrlImgPhotoIndividu) allow the construction of direct URLs to user profile images based solely on predictable identifiers such as user IDs and names. Due to missing authorization checks and lack of rate-limiting when generating or accessing these URLs, an unauthenticated or unauthorized actor may retrieve profile pictures of users by crafting requests with g

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized access to user profile images by exploiting predictable identifiers and the lack of proper access controls. This could lead to privacy violations, unauthorized data exposure, and potential misuse of personal information.
recommended_actions: Update INDEX-EDUCATION PRONOTE to version 2025.2.8 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement additional access control mechanisms, such as token-based authentication, to restrict access to sensitive resources.
Enable rate-limiting on endpoints to prevent abuse through automated or brute-force requests.

title: The `flow/admin/moniteur.php` script in Use It Flow administration website before 10.0.0 is...
description: The `flow/admin/moniteur.php` script in Use It Flow administration website before 10.0.0 is vulnerable to Remote Code Execution. When handling GET requests, the script takes user-supplied input from the `action` URL parameter, performs insufficient validation, and incorporates this input into a string that is subsequently executed by the `eval()` function. Although a `method_exists()` check is performed, it only validates the part of the user input *before* the first parenthesis `(`, allowing an

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code on the server, potentially leading to full system compromise, data theft, or service disruption. This poses a significant risk to any organization using the vulnerable version of the Use It Flow administration website.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Use It Flow administration website to version 10.0.0 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement input validation and sanitization for all user-supplied data to prevent exploitation of similar vulnerabilities in the future.
Conduct a security audit of the application to identify and address any additional vulnerabilities.

title: A write-what-where condition in p2r3 Bareiron commit 8e4d40 allows unauthenticated attackers to...
description: A write-what-where condition in p2r3 Bareiron commit 8e4d40 allows unauthenticated attackers to write arbitrary values to memory, enabling arbitrary code execution via a crafted packet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code by exploiting a write-what-where condition, potentially leading to full system compromise, data breaches, or service disruption.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest patch or update for p2r3 Bareiron if available.
Implement network-level protections such as intrusion detection and prevention systems (IDS/IPS) to monitor and block crafted packets targeting this vulnerability.
Conduct a thorough review of systems running p2r3 Bareiron to identify potential exploitation and ensure no unauthorized changes have occurred.

title: Out-of-bounds read in FFmpeg 8.0 and 8.0.1 RV60 video decoder (libavcodec/rv60dec.c). The...
description: Out-of-bounds read in FFmpeg 8.0 and 8.0.1 RV60 video decoder (libavcodec/rv60dec.c). The quantization parameter (qp) validation at line 2267 only checks the lower bound (qp < 0) but is missing upper bound validation. The qp value can reach 65 (base value 63 from 6-bit frame header + offset +2 from read_qp_offset) while the rv60_qp_to_idx array has size 64 (valid indices 0-63). This results in out-of-bounds array access at lines 1554 (decode_cbp8), 1655 (decode_cbp16), and 1419/1421 (get_c4x4_se

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2025-69693.

title: In Forgejo through 13.0.3, the attachment component allows a denial of service by uploading a...
description: In Forgejo through 13.0.3, the attachment component allows a denial of service by uploading a multi-gigabyte file attachment (e.g., to be associated with an issue or a release).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to perform a denial-of-service (DoS) attack by uploading excessively large files, potentially causing server resource exhaustion and downtime.
recommended_actions: Implement file size restrictions for attachments to prevent uploads of multi-gigabyte files.
Monitor and log file uploads to detect abnormal activity and enforce rate limiting.
Upgrade Forgejo to a patched version once available to mitigate the vulnerability.

title: CVE-2026-28693 - ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior
description: ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior to versions 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41, an integer overflow in DIB coder can result in out of bounds read or write. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28693.

title: CVE-2026-28692 - ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior
description: ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior to versions 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41, MAT decoder uses 32-bit arithmetic due to incorrect parenthesization resulting in a heap over-read. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in ImageMagick's MAT decoder could lead to a heap over-read, potentially allowing attackers to access sensitive memory data or cause application crashes. This could be exploited in scenarios where untrusted or malicious image files are processed.
recommended_actions: Upgrade ImageMagick to versions 7.1.2-16 or 6.9.13-41 immediately to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization for image files before processing them with ImageMagick.
Monitor and log the use of ImageMagick to detect any unusual or unauthorized activity involving image processing.

title: CVE-2026-28688 - ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior
description: ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior to versions 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41, a heap-use-after-free vulnerability exists in the MSL encoder, where a cloned image is destroyed twice. The MSL coder does not support writing MSL so the write capability has been removed. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41.

title: CVE-2026-28494 - ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior
description: ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior to versions 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41, a stack buffer overflow exists in ImageMagick's morphology kernel parsing functions. User-controlled kernel strings exceeding a buffer are copied into fixed-size stack buffers via memcpy without bounds checking, resulting in stack corruption. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28494.

All Misskey servers running versions 10.93.0 and later, but prior to 2026.3.1, contain a vulnerability that allows importing other users' data due to lack of ownership validation. The impact of this vulnerability is estimated to be relatively low, as bad actors would require the ID corresponding to the target file for import. This vulnerability is fixed in 2026.3.1.

title: CVE-2026-28431 - Misskey is an open source, federated social media platform. All Misskey servers running versions 8.4

All Misskey servers running versions 8.45.0 and later, but prior to 2026.3.1, contain a vulnerability that allows bad actors access to data that they ordinarily wouldn't be able to access due to insufficient permission checks and proper input validation. This vulnerability occurs regardless of whether federation is enabled or not. This vulnerability could lead to a significant data breach. This vulnerability is fixed in 2026.3.1.

title: CVE-2026-26982 - Ghostty is a cross-platform terminal emulator. Ghostty allows control characters such as 0x03 (Ctrl+
description: Ghostty is a cross-platform terminal emulator. Ghostty allows control characters such as 0x03 (Ctrl+C) in pasted and dropped text. These can be used to execute arbitrary commands in some shell environments. This attack requires an attacker to convince the user to copy and paste or drag and drop malicious text. The attack requires user interaction to be triggered, but the dangerous characters are invisible in most GUI environments so it isn't trivially detected, especially if the string contents are complex. Fixed in Ghostty v1.3.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-26982.

title: CVE-2026-1776 - Camaleon CMS versions 2.4.5.0 through 2.9.0, prior to commit f54a77e, contain a path traversal vulne
description: Camaleon CMS versions 2.4.5.0 through 2.9.0, prior to commit f54a77e, contain a path traversal vulnerability in the AWS S3 uploader implementation that allows authenticated users to read arbitrary files from the web server’s filesystem. The issue occurs in the download_private_file functionality when the application is configured to use the CamaleonCmsAwsUploader backend. Unlike the local uploader implementation, the AWS uploader does not validate file paths with valid_folder_path?, allowing directory traversal sequences to be supplied via the file parameter. As a result, any authenticated user, including low-privileged registered users, can access sensitive files such as /etc/passwd. This issue represents a bypass of the incomplete fix for CVE-2024-46987 and affects deployments using the AWS S3 storage backend.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-1776.
